title: Sticky Marmalade Whisky Chicken with Baked Spiced Rice
description: Oven-baked chicken thighs glazed in marmalade, whisky, lemon, and fennel seeds until sticky and golden, served over fragrant baked brown rice with shallots, caraway, almonds, and mint.

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C fan). Peel and halve the shallots, then slice each half into thin strips lengthways. Transfer to a deep oven-proof dish, drizzle generously with olive oil and sea salt, and bake for 15 minutes.

Make the chicken marinade: combine marmalade, whisky, lemon zest and juice, olive oil, caraway seeds, fennel seeds, and salt in a large bowl. Add chicken thighs and orange slices and toss well to coat everything. Transfer to a baking dish.
Toast caraway seeds for the rice in a dry pan over gentle heat for a couple of minutes until aromatic. Set aside.

Add rice to the shallot dish along with toasted caraway seeds, stir to combine. Pour over the warm stock and add butter, cinnamon stick, and star anise. Cover tightly with foil ensuring no air escapes. Place in the oven alongside the chicken.
Place chicken in the oven. Bake 35–40 minutes until sticky and golden. Check after 30 minutes — if the skin is browning too much, cover loosely with foil.

Remove rice from oven and leave covered to rest 5 minutes. Remove foil, discard cinnamon stick and star anise, and fluff with a fork. Season to taste, drizzle with olive oil, and top with almonds, mint leaves, and fresh lemon zest.
Transfer chicken to a platter and spoon over the marmalade juices from the pan. Serve alongside the spiced rice.

notes:
The baked rice method is worth trying even if you normally cook rice on the stovetop — it infuses a lot of flavor with minimal effort and works especially well when entertaining.

If you can't find banana shallots, regular shallots work fine -- just use 5-6 of them.

Whisky can be swapped for the juice of an orange for an alcohol-free version.
